name: Threat Intelligence IP Block Integration
summary: Integrates external threat intelligence feeds to automatically block known malicious IP ranges including attacker infrastructure, TOR exit nodes, and botnet sources.
objective: Reduce exposure to known hostile infrastructure by continuously ingesting curated IP intelligence and enforcing deny decisions at the WAF edge.
risk_mitigated: Automated exploitation from known attacker IPs
Abuse from TOR exit node traffic where policy disallows anonymity networks
Botnet-originated scanning, credential stuffing, and application-layer DDoS prep activity
assumptions: Organization has legal and policy approval to consume third-party threat feeds
WAF platform supports IP/CIDR block lists or equivalent reputation controls
Feed refresh and rollout workflows can be automated with rollback support

detection_logic.description: Match client source IP against dynamically maintained deny lists populated from external threat intelligence providers.
sources: Botnet C2 and infected node reputation feeds
normalization: Drop malformed, private, and reserved ranges unless explicitly approved

exceptions: Bypass for validated internal synthetic monitoring

tuning_notes: Begin in log-only mode for 3-7 days to baseline impact
Score and prioritize feeds by confidence and false-positive history
Set stricter action for high-confidence botnet and active exploitation feeds
Apply temporary quarantine (time-bound block) for lower-confidence sources
Maintain emergency global bypass and fast rollback for operational incidents
deployment_notes: Ingest feeds on a fixed cadence (e.g., every 15-60 minutes) with atomic list replacement
Version each generated list artifact for traceability
Validate feed freshness and fail closed/open according to risk policy
Promote through dev -> staging -> production with automated checks
Document feed ownership, SLA, and incident escalation path
monitoring_guidance: Track blocked request volume by feed/source and ASN
Alert on sudden spikes after feed updates
Measure false-positive rate via sampled log review and support tickets
Monitor list size growth and WAF platform capacity limits
Audit feed update success/failure and staleness age
limitations: IP reputation is ineffective against fast-rotating residential proxies
TOR-based blocking may affect privacy-conscious legitimate users
Third-party feed quality can vary and requires continuous validation
